title: Generalize the repro harness across 3 or more ecosystems
context: The M0 reproducible-environment harness (repro-004) was built for one ecosystem. M2 requires the harness to work across at least three ecosystems (e.g., Python/conda, R/renv, and a container-based tool) to enable imaging and single-cell contributions that use different toolchains. Scaling contributions across genomics, imaging, and survival-stats requires a single generalized harness that any contributor can extend with a documented 'add a new tool' path. Depends on repro-004.
objective: Extend the reproducible-environment harness to support at least three distinct ecosystems (e.g., Python/conda-lock, R/renv, and a container-based tool), each running a tutorial or test end-to-end in CI from its lockfile, with a documented path for adding new tools.
acceptanceCriteria: The harness runs reproducibly in CI for at least three ecosystems: e.g., Python/conda-lock or pixi, R/renv, and a container/Docker-based tool, each executing a tutorial or test end-to-end from its lockfile.
A documented 'add a new tool' path is committed, enabling contributors to plug in a new ecosystem lockfile without modifying the core harness.
Code is MIT-licensed; CI is green for all three ecosystems; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass for any Elyos-side tooling.
The generalized harness is the direct dependency for contrib-015 (imaging tutorial) and contrib-016 (single-cell docs + doctests).
resources: conda-lock/pixi documentation; renv documentation; Docker/OCI container documentation
output: A generalized MIT-licensed reproducible-environment harness supporting at least three ecosystems, with CI green across all and a documented extension path, enabling M2 contributions across genomics, imaging, and survival-stats toolchains.
